title: Welcome
body:
An interactive chapter for a Level 2+ vocational course. You will inspect a solar PV module, read its specifications, differentiate the panel types, and complete a knowledge check.

Click the blue “Begin module” button below to start.
instruction: Press play to start the video. Use the CC button for captions.

title: From a cell to a module
displayTitle: From a cell to a module
body: Electricity from the sun starts in a single solar cell. Wire many cells together and seal them, and you get a module.
instruction: Press play to start the video. Use the CC button for captions.

title: Inside the module
body: A solar PV module is a sealed sandwich built to survive 25 years outdoors.
instruction: Press play, then explore the 3D model and cross-section below.

_graphic.alt: Exploded cross-section of a solar PV module showing glass, EVA, cells, back-sheet, frame and junction box

title: The three technologies
body: There are three families of panel. Each has a different look, efficiency and best use.
instruction: Press play, then read the comparison below.

title: The three panel families
body: Swipe through the three panel families.

_items.title: Monocrystalline
_items.body: Cut from a single silicon crystal. Black, the most efficient, and the smallest area per watt — best where roof space is limited.

_items.title: Polycrystalline
_items.body: Cast from many crystals. Blue and speckled, a little less efficient, and the friendliest on price per watt when space is available.

_items.title: Thin-film
_items.body: Deposited semiconductor layers. Uniform and flexible, the lowest efficiency, but the calmest performer in extreme heat.

title: Reading specifications
body: Every panel carries a datasheet. The key numbers tell you how it behaves once wired into the system.
instruction: Press play, then read the spec guide below.

title: Reading the spec sheet
body: A panel rated 395 watt-peak (Wp) is measured at Standard Test Conditions (1000 W/m², 25 °C). The datasheet's key numbers — Pmax, Voc, Isc, Vmp and Imp, plus efficiency and the temperature coefficient — tell you how the panel behaves once it is wired into the system.

title: 3D virtual lab
body: Rotate the module, explode its layers, switch Mono / Poly / Thin-film, and change sunlight and temperature to watch live power output respond.

title: AR experience
displayTitle: View the panel in your space
body: Point your phone camera at a Hiro marker to place a full-size 3D panel in your space.
_fallback: AR works best on a mobile phone over HTTPS. On desktop, scan the QR code with your phone to launch the experience.

_qrCode._body: Scan this code with your phone camera. It opens the AR experience on your mobile device so you can place the panel in your space.
_qrCode._caption: Scan to open the AR experience on your phone

title: Interactive component lab
body: Four hands-on tools: build the module, size a system, explore temperature effects, and choose the right panel for a site.

title: System energy flow
body: Click each part of the rooftop system to learn its role, then switch between day and night to see how energy flows from panels to battery to household loads.

title: Panel parts and specifications
body: Tap each hotspot on the panel to identify the layer and see how the SolarMax-400W specification relates to real-world performance.

title: P-N junction simulation
body: Fire a photon into the silicon junction and watch the electric field separate the electron-hole pair to produce DC current.

title: Electrical safety lab
body: Three quick simulations: choose the right PPE, order the lockout/tagout steps, and select the correct multimeter category rating before touching a DC circuit.

title: Q1
body: Which layer of a solar PV module faces the sun and protects the cells?

_feedback.correct: Correct — low-iron tempered glass is the protective top layer.

_incorrect.final: Not quite. The sun-facing protective layer is the tempered front glass.

_partlyCorrect.final: Almost — the tempered front glass is the answer.

title: Q2
body: Which technology produces the most power per square metre?

_feedback.correct: Correct — the highest efficiency means the most power from the least area.

_incorrect.final: Monocrystalline is most efficient per m²; thin-film needs the most space.

title: Q3
body: Match each specification to its meaning.
instruction: Choose the correct meaning for each term, then Submit.

_feedback.correct: Correct — those are the three core datasheet values.

_incorrect.final: Review the datasheet: Pmax = max power, Voc = open-circuit voltage, Isc = short-circuit current.
